name: LIHEAP & Universal Service Fund (Energy Assistance)
org: NJ Dept. of Community Affairs / DCAid
summary: Helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills and can stop a utility shutoff. USF lowers your monthly electric and gas bill year-round; LIHEAP gives a seasonal benefit toward what you owe.
services: Help paying electric and gas bills
Emergency assistance to prevent or restore a shutoff
Monthly bill credits through USF
Medically-necessary cooling assistance

eligibility.summary: Low-to-moderate income NJ households; you don't need to be behind on the bill to apply, and enrolling can protect you from a winter shutoff.
eligibility.income: At or below 60% of state median income (FFY2026: about $6,732/mo for a household of 3, $8,014/mo for 4). USF also considers whether your energy bills are a high share of your income.
eligibility.residency: New Jersey resident responsible for your home heating or cooling costs.
eligibility.household: All household sizes; the income limit and benefit rise with household size.
eligibility.notes: Renters whose utilities are included in rent may still qualify. A citizen child's eligibility isn't affected by a parent's immigration status. Enrolling can trigger Winter Termination Program protection (no shutoff Nov 15–Mar 15).

requiredDocs: Photo ID for the applicant
Social Security cards for everyone in the household (birth certificate for infants under 12 months)
Proof of income for the last 4 weeks (pay stubs, or SSA/SSI/pension award letters); a Zero-Income Statement for any adult with no income
Proof you're responsible for the home: a current lease, or a mortgage or property-tax bill
All pages of your most recent gas and electric bills
Proof of U.S. citizenship or legal residency

location.address: Apply online at dcaid.dca.nj.gov; Hudson County in-person help at PACO, 390 Manila Ave

source: NJ DCA Division of Housing & Community Resources — FFY2026 USF/HEA factsheet and LIHEAP/USF application (nj.gov/dca/dhcr); local agency PACO. Verified Jun 2026.

name: SNAP (Food Assistance / NJ SNAP)
org: Hudson County Dept. of Family Services / Welfare Division
summary: Monthly money on a card to buy groceries. The amount depends on your household size and income, and benefits can start within days in an emergency.
services: Monthly grocery benefits on an EBT card
Expedited (emergency) SNAP within 7 days for very low income
Help applying and recertifying

eligibility.summary: Low-income households; most families with reduced hours or recent job loss qualify.
eligibility.income: Gross monthly income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level (Oct 2025–Sep 2026: about $4,109/mo for a household of 3, $4,957/mo for 4). Households with a member 60+ or who is disabled are exempt from the gross-income test.
eligibility.residency: Must live in Hudson County, NJ.
eligibility.household: Counts everyone who lives and eats together.
eligibility.notes: A U.S.-born or qualified-immigrant child can receive SNAP even if a parent is ineligible.

requiredDocs: Valid photo ID
Social Security number for each person applying
Proof of immigration status (for household members who aren't U.S. citizens)
Proof of income for the last 30 days (or proof of job loss / reduced hours)
Proof of resources, such as recent bank statements
Proof of Hudson County residence (a lease or utility bill)
source: NJ DHS Division of Family Development — NJ SNAP (nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap); Hudson County Dept. of Family Services. Verified Jun 2026.

name: WIC (Women, Infants & Children Nutrition)
org: City of Jersey City Dept. of Health & Human Services — WIC
summary: Free healthy food, formula, and nutrition support for pregnant people and kids under 5. Marisol's 5-year-old may still qualify until their 5th birthday.
services: Monthly benefits for milk, eggs, produce, formula, and cereal
Breastfeeding support
Nutrition and health referrals

eligibility.summary: Pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding people and children under age 5 with low-to-moderate income.
eligibility.income: At or below 185% of the federal poverty level (eff. June 2026: about $4,212/mo for a household of 3, $5,088/mo for 4). Being on SNAP, Medicaid, or TANF qualifies you automatically.
eligibility.household: Each eligible child and parent counts.
eligibility.notes: Open to families regardless of immigration status; using WIC does not affect immigration cases.

requiredDocs: Photo ID for the parent or guardian (and each applicant)
Proof of NJ residency (license, lease, or two pieces of recent mail)
Proof of income, OR a SNAP / Medicaid / TANF eligibility letter
The child's immunization records
Proof of pregnancy, if applying while pregnant
source: NJ Dept. of Health WIC (nj.gov/health/fhs/wic); City of Jersey City DHHS WIC clinic. Verified Jun 2026.

name: Triangle Park Community Center Food Pantry
summary: A neighborhood food pantry where you can pick up free groceries — no appointment and no income paperwork needed. Good for filling the fridge while longer applications process.
services: Free groceries and shelf-stable food
Fresh produce when available
Open to the community, no registration required

eligibility.summary: Open to anyone in need in the Jersey City area; no income or residency paperwork required to receive food.
eligibility.notes: No ID or immigration status required to receive groceries; bringing a photo ID and your own bags is helpful.

requiredDocs: None required to receive food
A photo ID and your own bags are helpful but optional
source: Triangle Park Community Center (triangleparkcc.org); City of Jersey City certified food-pantry list. Verified Jun 2026 — confirm hours by phone.

name: NJ SHARES Emergency Utility Grant
summary: A one-time grant to help households that are slightly over the income limit for LIHEAP but still can't cover an electric or gas bill and face a shutoff.
services: One-time grant paid directly to the utility
Help for households just above other program limits

eligibility.summary: For households in a temporary energy crisis whose income is just above the LIHEAP/USF limit.
eligibility.income: Income above the LIHEAP/USF limit but not over 400% of the federal poverty level; you can't have received LIHEAP this program year.
eligibility.residency: New Jersey resident with a residential utility account in your name.
eligibility.notes: You must have made a good-faith payment of at least $100 in the last 90 days, and apply to LIHEAP and USF first. Grants (up to $700) are paid directly to the utility.

requiredDocs: Photo ID for the applicant and all household members
Proof of residency
Proof of income for the last 4 consecutive weeks (or a zero-income statement)
Your most recent energy bill
Your LIHEAP and USF determination letters
Proof of a good-faith payment of at least $100 in the last 90 days

location.address: Phone and online intake; 200+ partner agencies statewide

source: New Jersey SHARES energy assistance guidelines (sharesnation.org). Verified Jun 2026.

name: WorkFirst NJ / TANF (Temporary Cash Assistance)
org: Hudson County Dept. of Family Services / Welfare Division
summary: Short-term monthly cash for families with children who have little or no income, plus help finding work and emergency assistance for rent or utilities.
services: Monthly cash assistance for families with children
Emergency Assistance for rent or utility crises
Job training and child-care support

eligibility.summary: Families with children and very low income and resources.
eligibility.income: Very low income (monthly limit about $839 for a household of 3, rising with size); resources at or below $2,000.
eligibility.residency: Hudson County resident; U.S. citizen or qualified non-citizen.
eligibility.notes: Time-limited; comes with work requirements once stabilized. The local office also offers Emergency Assistance for rent or utility crises.

requiredDocs: Photo ID
Birth certificates for the children
Proof of income and resources
Proof of Hudson County address
Social Security numbers for household members
source: NJ DHS WorkFirst NJ (nj.gov/humanservices/wfnj); Hudson County Dept. of Family Services. Verified Jun 2026.

name: Child Care Assistance Program (Child Care Subsidy)
org: NJ Division of Family Development; Hudson CCR&R: Urban League of Hudson County
summary: Helps working or job-searching parents pay for child care so they can keep or look for a job. Covers part of the cost at approved providers.
services: Help paying for licensed child care
Coverage while working, training, or job searching

eligibility.summary: Working or job-searching parents with low-to-moderate income and children under 13.
eligibility.income: At or below 250% of the federal poverty level to start, with a one-year phase-out up to 85% of state median income; priority for families at or below 100% of poverty.
eligibility.household: Children from birth through age 13 (up to 19 with special needs).
eligibility.notes: A recent job loss with an active job search can still qualify you. Enrollment reopened in 2026 with a statewide cap, so apply early.

requiredDocs: Photo ID
Children's birth certificates or proof of age
Proof of income
Proof you need care (work, school, or training)
Proof of NJ address

location.address: Urban League of Hudson County, 253 Martin Luther King Jr. Dr

source: NJ DHS Division of Family Development — CCAP (childcarenj.gov); Urban League of Hudson County (CCR&R). Verified Jun 2026.

name: Emergency Assistance — Rent & Eviction Prevention
summary: Short-term help for Hudson County renters at risk of eviction — emergency aid and connection to rental assistance and housing counseling.
services: Short-term emergency aid to prevent eviction
Help understanding your options
Referrals to rental assistance and legal aid

eligibility.summary: Low-income Hudson County residents at risk of losing their housing.
eligibility.income: Low income; exact limits vary by program — call to confirm.
eligibility.notes: Not a legal determination — staff help you understand options. Exact eligibility and documents vary, so call before you go.

requiredDocs: Photo ID
Current lease
Any late-rent or eviction notice received
Proof of income and of the hardship (e.g., a reduced-hours letter)

location.address: Call for the office nearest you

source: North Hudson Community Action Corp — Emergency Assistance Dept (nhcac.org). Verified Jun 2026; confirm eligibility and documents by phone.

name: Northeast NJ Legal Services
summary: Free legal help for low-income residents on utility shutoffs, evictions, and denied benefits. They can talk to your utility company or appear in housing court for you.
services: Help disputing a utility shutoff
Eviction defense
Appeals when a benefit is wrongly denied

eligibility.summary: Low-income residents of Hudson, Bergen, and Passaic counties.
eligibility.income: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level.
eligibility.residency: Serves Hudson, Bergen, and Passaic counties.
eligibility.notes: Provides legal advice and representation — Compass does not.

requiredDocs: Photo ID
Any court papers, shutoff notice, or denial letter
Proof of income
source: Northeast New Jersey Legal Services (nnjls.org), part of the LSNJ network; statewide hotline 1-888-576-5529. Verified Jun 2026.

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
summary: Free, confidential support 24/7 for anyone in emotional distress or crisis — for you or someone you care about. Call or text any time.
services: 24/7 crisis counseling by call or text
Support for emotional distress, not only suicide
Connection to local mental-health services

eligibility.summary: Anyone, any time. No eligibility requirements.
eligibility.notes: If someone is in immediate danger, call 911. For Spanish, press 2 when you call, or text AYUDA.

requiredDocs: None
source: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988lifeline.org) and SAMHSA. Verified Jun 2026.

name: SNAP & Temporary Assistance (Bergen County)
org: Bergen County Board of Social Services
summary: Bergen County's office for food assistance (SNAP) and temporary cash help for residents who live in Bergen rather than Hudson County.
services: SNAP food benefits
Temporary cash assistance
General assistance for single adults

eligibility.income: Gross monthly income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level (Oct 2025–Sep 2026: about $4,109/mo for a household of 3, $4,957/mo for 4).
eligibility.residency: Must live in Bergen County, NJ.
eligibility.notes: Apply here only if you live in Bergen County; Hudson residents use the Hudson office.

requiredDocs: Photo ID
Proof of income for the last 30 days
Proof of Bergen County address
Social Security numbers for applicants
source: Bergen County Board of Social Services (bcbss.com); NJ SNAP FFY2026 income limits. Verified Jun 2026.

name: Center for Food Action Pantry (Bergen)
summary: Emergency groceries and help with rent and utility emergencies for Bergen County families, with same-day food available.
services: Emergency food packages
Help with one-time rent or utility emergencies
Holiday and school-supply support

eligibility.summary: Bergen County (and northern Passaic) residents in a food or financial emergency.
eligibility.residency: Serves Bergen County and northern Passaic; cannot serve Paterson or Passaic city.
eligibility.notes: Register at the pantry or by phone. Income limits aren't published — anyone in the service area who is in need can get food.

requiredDocs: A photo ID and proof of address are helpful
For rent or utility help: the bill or notice and proof of income
source: Center for Food Action (cfanj.org). Verified Jun 2026.
